title: eToro Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

content:
I'll write a comprehensive pricing deep-dive for eToro. Let me create the HTML content with all required sections.

```html

eToro Pricing Overview

eToro's headline is simple: zero account fees. The platform charges no monthly account fee, no sign-up fee, and no platform fee to open a Standard Account, eToro Club Silver, or eToro Club Gold+. All three tiers start at $0/month. This is a major draw for entry-level traders, but the "free" label masks a complex fee structure that catches many users off guard. While base trading on stocks and ETFs is commission-free, eToro generates revenue through currency conversion spreads, withdrawal fees, inactivity penalties, and leveraged trading markups that can significantly impact total cost of ownership—especially for frequent traders or international users.

All eToro Plans Compared

eToro offers a tiered account structure, but the tiers are more about status and rewards than about unlocking core trading features. All users gain access to the same markets (stocks, ETFs, crypto, forex, commodities, options) and the same core tool—CopyTrader—immediately upon signup.

Account Tier Monthly Cost Annual Cost Best For
Standard Account $0 $0 New traders, small portfolios under $5,000
eToro Club Silver $0 $0 Active traders wanting modest bonuses (thresholds vary by region)
eToro Club Gold+ $0 $0 High-volume traders seeking priority support and performance rebates

What this means in practice: Upgrading from Standard to Silver or Gold+ requires maintaining a minimum balance and/or trading volume (these thresholds vary by region and are subject to change). Once you qualify, you unlock perks like quarterly bonus cash, cashback on spreads, and access to eToro's trading signal community—but you pay nothing extra in membership fees. The tiers are earned through activity, not purchased.

Free Plan / Free Trial

eToro's Standard Account is the free tier, and there is no separate "trial" period. You open a real account immediately with real money (minimum $10 deposit recommended, though some regions allow lower minimums). There is also a demo account available at signup, which is fully free and uses virtual money to let you paper-trade and learn before funding a live account.

Free tier limitations:

  • Commission-free trading on stocks and ETFs (no hidden charge here)
  • Access to 35+ million users and the full CopyTrader library
  • Paper trading via demo account for unlimited practice
  • Mobile app with full charting and alerts included
  • Not limited: You can trade crypto, forex, commodities, and options from day one if you deposit real funds

Is the free tier worth it? Yes, for beginners. The demo account is genuinely useful for learning without risk, and the live Standard Account imposes no monthly penalty if you trade lightly. However, read the fine print below—the fee structure can surprise you.

Hidden Costs and Fees

eToro's $0 account fee masks several material costs that add up fast:

Withdrawal Fees

Every withdrawal incurs a $5 flat fee, regardless of amount. This is one of eToro's most painful charges. If you withdraw monthly, that's $60/year. If you withdraw weekly, it's $260/year. Even a quarterly withdrawal costs $20/year. This fee hits passive investors and portfolio rebalancers especially hard.

Inactivity Fee

If your account is inactive for 12 consecutive months, eToro charges $10/month until you trade again or close the account. "Inactive" means no trades, transfers, or account updates. If you open an account and leave $5,000 parked there without touching it, you'll pay $120/year in inactivity fees until you log back in. This penalizes buy-and-hold investors and people who accumulate positions over time.

Currency Conversion and Spread Costs

Non-USD deposits incur currency conversion fees (typically 1-2% depending on your payment method and region). On forex and crypto trades, eToro charges spreads—not commissions, but the bid-ask gap is wider than at professional platforms. For example, on EUR/USD, spreads average 2-3 pips; on Bitcoin, spreads can exceed 1%. These aren't listed as "fees," but they reduce your effective buying power.

Margin and Leverage Costs

If you trade on margin or use leverage (available on forex and CFDs), eToro charges overnight funding costs, typically 2-8% annually depending on the asset and your position direction. US traders cannot access leveraged forex or stock CFDs due to regulatory restrictions, but international users should budget for these costs if using leverage.

Premium Feature Costs

CopyTrader is free, but copying certain high-performing investors (Popular Investors) may incur performance fees—typically 5-20% of profits generated from that copy. This is optional, but it's a cost if you choose to copy a premium trader.

eToro Pricing vs Competitors

How does eToro stack up against alternatives? Here's a snapshot of the competitive landscape:

Broker Account Fee Stock Commission Withdrawal Fee Key Advantage
eToro $0 $0 $5 per withdrawal CopyTrader, social trading, multi-asset
Interactive Brokers $0 (or $10/month if under $25K AUM) $0 (stocks) $0 Advanced tools, professional-grade charting, lowest spreads
Fidelity $0 $0 $0 Excellent education, no inactivity fees, strong research
E-TRADE $0 $0 $0 Balanced platform, educational resources, good mobile app

Where eToro wins on price: No account minimums, no inactivity fees if you trade regularly, and commission-free trading on stocks/ETFs matches competitors. CopyTrader has no direct competitor—it's a unique social trading feature.

Where eToro loses: The $5 withdrawal fee is punitive compared to free withdrawals at Fidelity, E-TRADE, and most others. Interactive Brokers has tighter spreads and lower overall costs for active forex and derivatives traders. Fidelity has zero inactivity fees and better educational resources for beginners.

Note: Competitor pricing is approximate and subject to regional variation. Always verify current rates on competitor websites before opening an account.

Is eToro Worth the Price?

eToro pricing is worth it if:

  • You're a beginner who wants to learn by copying experienced traders. CopyTrader is genuinely valuable and has no real competitor—the fee structure is worth the learning advantage.
  • You're a multi-asset investor who wants stocks, crypto, and forex in a single platform. Consolidation saves hassle even if spreads aren't the tightest.
  • You plan to deposit once and hold for years without frequent withdrawals. The $10 inactivity fee doesn't apply if you trade at least once per 12 months.
  • You live outside the US and want regulated access to forex and commodities. eToro is FCA-regulated and available in 100+ countries.

eToro pricing is NOT worth it if:

  • You're a frequent withdrawer (weekly or bi-weekly). At $5 per withdrawal, you'll pay $260-$520/year—a hidden cost rival brokers don't impose.
  • You want professional-grade charting and order flow analysis. eToro's charts are basic and lack custom indicators; Interactive Brokers or thinkorswim are better.
  • You're a high-frequency trader or forex scalper. eToro's spreads (2-3 pips on major pairs) are wider than institutional competitors—you'll bleed money on volume.
  • You're a US trader seeking forex leverage. Regulatory restrictions eliminate an entire asset class; Forex.com or OANDA may be better.

Value for different trader types:

  • Passive/copy traders: Excellent value. CopyTrader's zero fee for replicating trades is unbeatable for hands-off investing.
  • Stock-and-ETF investors: Fair value. Zero commissions match competitors, but the $5 withdrawal fee stings if you rebalance frequently.
  • Crypto traders: Decent value. Spreads on major coins are reasonable, and the social community is valuable for research.
  • Forex day traders: Poor value. Spreads are too wide, and overnight funding costs add up fast.

How to Save on eToro

Reduce your total cost of ownership:

  • Minimize withdrawals. Batch withdrawals quarterly instead of monthly to cut $5 fees from $60/year down to $20/year. Keep emergency cash in your eToro account if possible.
  • Avoid the inactivity penalty. Make at least one trade per calendar year—even a $10 stock purchase counts. This eliminates the $10/month fee.
  • Use the demo account to learn. The free paper trading environment is fully featured. Log 50+ hours of practice before funding a live account to avoid costly beginner mistakes.
  • Copy traders with transparent track records. The most expensive Popular Investors charge 5-20% performance fees. Focus on free CopyTrader opportunities or low-fee traders to avoid profit leakage.
  • Deposit in USD if possible. Currency conversion fees on non-USD deposits (1-2%) can be avoided by holding USD or using a multi-currency payment method.
  • Avoid margin trading if you're new. Overnight funding costs and forced liquidations are expensive lessons. Stick to fully-funded positions until you understand leverage.

No official discount codes: eToro does not offer public promotional codes or seasonal discounts (as of 2026). Bonus promotions are region-specific and typically restricted to new accounts in select markets. Always check eToro's website for your region's current offers.

```
